heading: 9.7.16.1.1.Tensor Memory Addressing
content:
Tensor Memory addresses are 32-bit wide and specify two components.

1. Lane index
2. Column index

The layout is as follows:

> |  |  |
> | --- | --- |
> | 31 16 | 15 0 |
> | Lane index | Column index |

[Figure 182](#tensor-memory-layout) shows the view of the Tensor Memory Layout within CTA.



[Figure: tensor-memory-layout]
TENSOR MEMORY LAYOUT AND ADDRESSING

OVERVIEW:
This figure shows a 2D tensor memory layout organized as a grid of lanes (rows) and columns, with explicit address values for each element position. The layout is row-major, where each lane (row) contains a contiguous 2KB block of memory spanning 512 columns.

DIMENSIONS:
- Number of lanes (rows): 128 (Lane 0 through Lane 127)
- Number of columns per lane: 512 (Col 0 through Col 511)
- Size per lane: 2KB (2 kilobytes)
- Total memory: 128 lanes x 2KB = 256KB

VISUAL STRUCTURE:
Each lane is represented as a wide horizontal orange/yellow rectangle labeled "2KB" in the center. The lanes are stacked vertically from Lane 0 at the top to Lane 127 at the bottom. Between Lane 2 and Lane 127, an ellipsis (...) indicates the intermediate lanes are not shown.

COLUMN LABELS (top of figure):
Col 0, Col 1, Col 2, ..., Col 511

ADDRESS FORMAT:
Addresses are expressed in the format 0xLLLL.CCCC where:
- LLLL = lane number in hexadecimal (upper 16 bits)
- CCCC = column number in hexadecimal (lower 16 bits)

ADDRESS VALUES BY LANE:

Lane 0 (lane index 0x0000):
- Col 0: 0x0000.0000
- Col 1: 0x0000.0001
- Col 2: 0x0000.0002
- ...
- Col 511: 0x0000.01FF

Lane 1 (lane index 0x0001):
- Col 0: 0x0001.0000
- Col 1: 0x0001.0001
- Col 2: 0x0001.0002
- ...
- Col 511: 0x0001.01FF

Lane 2 (lane index 0x0002):
- Col 0: 0x0002.0000
- Col 1: 0x0002.0001
- Col 2: 0x0002.0002
- ...
- Col 511: 0x0002.01FF

Lane 127 (lane index 0x007F):
- Col 0: 0x007F.0000
- Col 1: 0x007F.0001
- Col 2: 0x007F.0002
- ...
- Col 511: 0x007F.01FF

ADDRESS CALCULATION PATTERN:
Address = (lane_number << 16) | column_number
- Lane number occupies bits [31:16] (upper 16 bits)
- Column number occupies bits [15:0] (lower 16 bits)
- Column 511 = 0x01FF confirms 9-bit column addressing (512 columns = 2^9)
- Each lane stride = 0x00010000 (65536 in decimal)
- Each column stride within a lane = 0x00000001 (1 unit)

DIRECTIONAL ARROWS:
- A horizontal blue arrow points left-to-right along the column dimension (between Lane 0 and Lane 1 rows), indicating increasing column addresses from Col 0 to Col 511
- A vertical blue arrow points top-to-bottom along the lane dimension (at the Col 0 position), indicating increasing lane addresses from Lane 0 to Lane 127

DATA ORGANIZATION:
- Row-major layout: columns within a lane are contiguous in address space
- Each lane is a separate 2KB row with addresses starting at 0xLLLL.0000 and ending at 0xLLLL.01FF
- Lanes are separated by a fixed stride of 0x00010000 in the address space
- The addressing scheme encodes both the lane (row) and column index directly into the address word


Figure 182 Tensor Memory Layout and Addressing
